text.title: Prometheus Monitoring Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Prometheus pod OOM-restarts hourly; memory usage climbs after every deploy.
antipattern.reason: user_id or request_id used as a label; each unique value spawns a new time series.
antipattern.note: Fix: Strip unbounded labels at scrape time via metric_relabel_configs drop rules. Move per-request data to traces, not metrics.

antipattern.description: On-call paged 8 times per night for pod restarts, none impacted users.
antipattern.reason: Alerts fire on causes (KubePodCrashLooping) not symptoms (5xx rate up).
antipattern.note: Fix: Rewrite alerts to fire on symptoms: error rate, latency p99, saturation. Add runbook_url to every alert.

antipattern.description: Dashboard shows a sawtooth pattern that resets every deploy; Grafana panel is unreadable.
antipattern.reason: Counter value plotted directly; counter resets on process restart.
antipattern.note: Fix: Wrap in rate(): rate(http_requests_total[5m]). For percentages, sum() by () then divide.

antipattern.description: p99 latency averaged across 10 replicas is meaningless; numbers don't match SLO targets.
antipattern.reason: Summary used instead of histogram; per-instance quantiles cannot be aggregated.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace summary with histogram. Use histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(latency_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le)).

antipattern.description: Grafana dashboard takes 30s to load; Prometheus CPU pegged.
antipattern.reason: Dashboard runs expensive aggregations on every panel refresh.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move aggregation to recording rules with naming pattern level:metric:operation (job:http_requests:rate5m).
